readyToPublish.title: What the Running System Found
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 15. Jun 9+11: 6/9 agents silently drifted from logging discipline — all self-healed same day; cron-halt mystery empirically diagnosed and resolved; Routines watchdog decision triggered. Through-line: the running system surfaces what the migration could not see.

readyToPublish.title: Almost Beta
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 16. Jun 12-14: re-migration wave (DinP/Sonnet); M3 gate CLOSED; PM declaration: 'alpha — almost beta — Piper Morgan is a good PM assistant.' Through-line: the declared milestone and what it actually means.

readyToPublish.title: The Ritual Becomes a Skill
readyToPublish.notes: Insight developed by PM with ChatGPT (separate side project extracting the blog's cartoon-illustration process into a portable skill, ahead of losing access to the account it evolved in) -- delivered 2026-07-18 as a truncated export stub, full manuscript pasted in same day. Through-line: a tacit creative collaboration (a year of iterative cartoon-drafting) gets extracted into a durable artifact (a skill) via an implicit eval loop -- examples, failures, criteria, pattern language -- before the memory holding it disappears. Comms full review 2026-07-18: fixed 2 real semicolons, converted section breaks to H1 per series convention, verified 2 of 3 self-referential cartoon examples against real published alt text (storm-window/Server-Crashed-Mid-Draft, ship-shipyard/Migration-Wave both confirmed exact matches); 1 genuine sequence error found (draft said the 'one train + map' concept was the final rendered image for the documentation-drift cartoon, but published alt text confirms it was the 'two trains diverging' concept) -- PM confirmed ChatGPT had flipped the sequence, corrected. Slotted into Jul 25 (bumping 'From Abstraction to Worked Example' to Sep 19) as the strongest thematic pairing found in the queue -- both pieces argue concrete examples beat abstract description, in different domains. Filename renamed from Title-Case to lowercase-hyphenated per project convention (PM-approved). Footer chain repaired end-to-end (Almost Beta -> this piece -> The Meta-Observation Pattern). Frontmatter (image/alt/caption) still empty, no art made yet. Still needs PM voice-pass proper (this was a mechanical/factual pass, not a voice edit).

readyToPublish.title: The Meta-Observation Pattern
readyToPublish.notes: Insight rescued from orphan state 2026-05-29 (drafted Apr 26, untracked until Docs cleanup pass). Through-line: three pieces published that week all described coordination while being part of coordination — the system-using-itself-to-write-about-itself pattern. Carries in-body CONSIDER flag re self-observation arc density; PM ratified KEEP 2026-05-24. Needs PM voice-pass (1 ADD + 1 CONSIDER) + frontmatter before publish. Pairs with From Abstraction to Worked Example (Sat Jul 25) as communication-craft weekend.

readyToPublish.title: The Trust Architecture Hardens
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 17. Jun 15-19: three ADRs (066/070/071) ratified in 5 days + Lead self-correction praised as "the discipline at its best"; fire-as-wake-not-timebox methodology fix ships team-wide; app-shell migration finishes (22/22 pages) + ADR-072 authored-to-ratified same day; Radar-swap 7 issues in 2h + StandupAssembler fabrication caught by PM UAT (hard-gap vs soft-gap distinction named) + battery outage kills all scheduled processes; D1 sprint closes (7 issues, 221 clean test conversations) + role-portfolio wave launches same afternoon. Through-line: scattered vigilance rules harden into tested, ratified architecture across 5 days. Researched directly against the 5 source omnibus logs (not the prior survey pass). 1 PM voice-pass marker (personal reaction to the fabrication catch). Pairs forward with RECONNECT's Keystone (Jul 30).

readyToPublish.title: RECONNECT's Keystone
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 18. Jun 20-28: Connector protocol (#1232) ratified as RECONNECT keystone same evening a real security hole (writable unauthenticated admin route) is found and closed hours later; v0.8.9 alpha shipped + deployed with an encryption env-var bug caught and fixed same day; PM's own phone UAT catches a broken onboarding health-check; external tester Jake's install feedback closes same-day (fast-loop, not first-contact - corrected against glossary v1.1 which shows Jun 9 distribution); Architect owns a public self-correction (m-30, mistook instantiated-for-called) same day Lead's trace disproves the earlier ruling; GitHub+Calendar go live through the new protocol. Ends on "proved itself, not yet claiming more" - deliberately does not resolve the beta-scope question (that's a later beat). Researched directly against 9 source omnibus logs. 3 PM voice-pass markers (framing word, personal anecdote, Jake timeline sign-off). Pairs back with The Trust Architecture Hardens (Jul 28).

readyToPublish.title: Mechanism Beats Vigilance
readyToPublish.notes: Insight from the duty-cycle cron-rule split (May 28). Principle: promote vigilance-disciplines to mechanisms; promote per failure-mode not per surface-rule. Pairs Aug 1/2 with The Architecture That Wrote Its Own Case (mechanism-family). First draft 2026-06-04 (subagent + Comms voice-pass); 1 PM-voice-pass marker. Footer provisional (re-verify at publish). Syndication: Medium+LinkedIn.

readyToPublish.title: The Architecture That Wrote Its Own Case
readyToPublish.notes: Insight abstracted from Beat 11 narrative (kept distinct, principle-altitude): some problems are architectural not discipline-fixable; the evidence writes its own case. Pairs Aug 1/2 w/ Mechanism Beats Vigilance. PM-voice-pass flag inline: middle mechanism-beats-vigilance section overlaps the paired standalone piece - consider compressing. Footer provisional.

readyToPublish.title: The List That Lies
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 19. Two unrelated trust-boundary failures (Piper's confabulated "Milestone created," then a security-perimeter regression) fixed with escalating rigor -- list (broke) -> derive-from-registry (held) -> derive-and-prove-with-a-test (invite-token system, shipped day of ratification). Drafted + fact-checked against primary logs 2026-07-09 (subagent + Comms review); fixed 3 negation-reveal-cliche instances the skill's own grep missed on first pass (regex tightened in template-audit v1.1). 1 PM-input bracket (who made the undocumented Jun 29 perimeter edit). Footer chains to Beat 20 (Aug 6).

readyToPublish.title: Drained on Paper
readyToPublish.notes: Beat 20. Ship #049's "fully drained" language (traced to Lead Dev's Jul 1 log, not the Ship itself) proven wrong by PM's Jul 4 gut-check (2/8 connectors live end to end); depth-first pivot; PPM's 1175-item Sprint-field wipe tied by PM directly to Piper's own confabulation problem. Drafted + fact-checked against primary logs 2026-07-09; fixed 2 negation-reveal-cliche instances. 1 PM-input bracket (dishes/baby analogy exact wording). Footer chains to "Verify at the User Path" (Aug 8) -- took over that link from "The Architecture That Wrote Its Own Case," which now chains to Beat 19 instead.

readyToPublish.title: Verify at the User Path, Not the Data Layer
readyToPublish.notes: Insight from #1047 /insights break (May 29-30): DB-verified != user-can-load-it; verify the user actual path. Pairs Aug 8/9 w/ Over-Checking (verification-family). First draft 2026-06-04. Footer provisional.

readyToPublish.title: Over-Checking Has Dividends
readyToPublish.notes: Insight abstracted from Beat 12 (kept distinct): the dividend of over-checking is the bug that never shipped - invisible because nothing went wrong; skill is knowing when the asymmetry flips. Pairs Aug 8/9 w/ Verify-at-User-Path. Footer provisional.

readyToPublish.title: Confabulating a Peer Unfinished Work
readyToPublish.notes: Insight from June 1 cohort confabulation catch (CXO flagged a PPM agent asserting unfinished peer work): source-discipline scales from dont-lie-to-user to dont-let-team-lie-to-itself; flag-dont-paper-over. Solo Aug 15. Footer TBD (re-verify at publish). PM-flagged most-timely.

readyToPublish.title: The Fabricating Standup
readyToPublish.notes: Insight candidate A (surfaced Jun 20, drafted Jul 4). Jun 18: a carefully built, honestly-derived standup engine was fully wired but a classifier/handler mismatch let a request silently fall through to an LLM fallback that fabricated a fluent, confident, false status report. Root cause: Pattern-045 (Green Tests, Red User) - untested end-to-end seam. Triggered a systematic routing-mismatch audit (#1283) rather than a one-off patch. Needs PM voice-pass (1 FACT-CHECK + 1 ADD PERSONAL ANECDOTE). Pairs with Confabulating a Peer Unfinished Work (Aug 15) on the confabulation theme.

readyToPublish.title: The Trust Gate That Wasn't
readyToPublish.notes: Insight candidate B (surfaced Jun 20, drafted Jul 4). Jun 17: a TrustStage gate meant to govern Piper’s proactive behavior was mistakenly wired into navigation visibility, hiding the user’s own saved Lists behind a trust-stage threshold. PM caught it looking for his own lists; principle ratified into ADR-072 D5 (Piper-initiated = gate-eligible, user-reaching-for-own = never gated). Needs PM voice-pass (1 FACT-CHECK on Lists/Collections naming + 1 CONSIDER).

readyToPublish.title: Read the Mock First
readyToPublish.notes: Insight candidate C (surfaced Jun 20, drafted Jul 4). Jun 17-19: a nav-shell rebuild followed a ratified written spec but drifted from the actual mockup’s minimal intent (only the home page was mocked; other pages’ nav content was guessed). PM UAT caught the drift twice; PM chose spec-first over patch-or-revert; rebuild against the actual mock (not the spec) passed re-UAT same day. Needs PM voice-pass (1 FACT-CHECK on quoting UAT verdict + 1 ADD PERSONAL ANECDOTE + 1 CONSIDER).

readyToPublish.title: The Orphan Migration
readyToPublish.notes: Insight candidate D (surfaced Jun 20, drafted Jul 4). Jun 17: one DB table (project_integrations) had never had a create migration, protected by defensive later-migrations that silently no-op’d on a fresh database. Fix went at the front of the migration chain (not mid-chain) to repair both fresh and already-current databases. A new structural guard (scan models vs. migrations) then independently caught 4 more tables with the same latent bug on its first run. Needs PM voice-pass (1 FACT-CHECK on naming revision IDs + 1 CONSIDER).

readyToPublish.title: Two of Me
readyToPublish.notes: Insight candidate E (surfaced Jun 20, drafted Jul 4). Jun 19: two Lead Dev sessions ran in parallel unaware of each other after a laptop battery death + scheduled wake-up forked what should have been one continuous session. Nothing lost because both sessions independently followed push-often discipline. Principle: exactly one coordinator at a time; whatever revives a coordinating session must land in a fresh space, never share a room with a session that might already be running. Needs PM voice-pass (1 FACT-CHECK on the unresolved trigger mechanism). Last drafted post in the queue as of Jul 4 - footer intentionally open pending the next beat.

readyToPublish.title: We Built Onboarding in Our Own Image
readyToPublish.notes: Insight from BYOC cold-start interview testing May 19-31. PM built the onboarding serial-question-by-question because that is PM's personal working preference for ongoing use. The mode that earns trust in a working relationship turned out to be wrong for first-encounter onboarding — stretched experience, no map, no sense of how much was left. Through-line: building onboarding in your own image imports the assumptions of someone who has already crossed the threshold.

readyToPublish.title: Patterns Naming Patterns
readyToPublish.notes: Insight rescued from orphan state 2026-07-04 (fully drafted with detailed sourcing, but never got a calendar row; original footer referenced Extension Without Integration as next, which published 2026-06-21 before this was ever slotted). May 9-11: Lead Dev filed Pattern-067 (Issue-Body Reality Mismatch); CIO independently filed a different Pattern-067 (Silent State Mutation) May 11, unaware of the collision; Architect caught it at merge; first-filed-wins renumbering (CIO -> 068+069) resolved in ~30 min; slot-allocation check added to filing convention. Through-line: methodology written for the product surface needs explicit translation to the methodology surface itself, or the catalog cannot catch its own failure modes. Carries 2 detailed FACT-CHECK/SOURCE-NEEDED notes already substantially self-answered with citations - likely just need PM confirmation, not fresh research.

readyToPublish.title: No Undo
readyToPublish.notes: Irreversible actions need their own guardrail class, separate from routine competence -- named after 3 destructive-command incidents in 2 weeks (PA's sprint-wipe, PPM's 1175-item wipe, Lead Dev's actual `docker volume rm` execution on shared dev Postgres, confirmed via primary log as executed not a near-miss), tied by PM directly to Piper's own confabulation problem. Drafted + fact-checked 2026-07-09; fixed 2 negation-reveal-cliche instances. 1 PM-input bracket (babysitter/baby analogy attribution). Footer chains to "Assume It Was You" (Sep 13).

readyToPublish.title: Assume It Was You
readyToPublish.notes: Context discontinuity can look exactly like a phantom peer -- the Architect's false "two sessions must be running" alarm, root-caused to losing memory of its own prior actions and misreading its own fresh commits as an intruder's. Now a standing CLAUDE.md default (check your own log before hypothesizing a peer). Drafted + fact-checked 2026-07-09 against docs/internal/operations/duty-cycle-self-attribution-drift-2026-07-06.md; fixed 2 negation-reveal-cliche instances. 1 PM-input bracket (whether the "compaction hand-wave rejection" paragraph is PM's own words or the Architect's account of PM). Last post in the current insight queue as of Jul 9 -- footer flagged for re-verification once more is scheduled.

readyToPublish.title: From Abstraction to Worked Example
readyToPublish.notes: Insight rescued from orphan state 2026-05-29 (drafted Apr 26, untracked until Docs cleanup pass). Through-line: three concrete worked examples + one contrast made Lead Dev's ethics-enforcement architectural choice click in two minutes when descriptions wouldn't — the abstraction-clicks-via-worked-example method. Generalizes to API design, methodology patterns, user-facing copy. Needs PM voice-pass (1 ADD + 1 CONSIDER placeholder) + frontmatter before publish. Pairs with Meta-Observation Pattern (Sun Jul 26) as communication-craft weekend. Bumped from 2026-07-25 to 2026-09-19 (2026-07-18, PM-confirmed) to make room for 'The Ritual Becomes a Skill,' a stronger thematic pairing for that slot (both pieces hinge on 'concrete examples over abstraction'). Footer retargeted to a last-in-queue placeholder pending the next scheduled beat; Almost Beta's footer now points to the Ritual piece instead.

recentlyPublished.title: The Package and the First Bite
recentlyPublished.notes: Beat 12. May 29 v0.7.0 adoption package + Web sub-hour bite + log-currency clock->event flip; May 30 over-check dividend on #1016 + write-it-to-a-file. Through-line: distribute the new way + over-checking has dividends. First draft 2026-06-03; needs PM voice-pass. Footer teases When the Documentation Drifts (Jul 11). Comms full review 2026-07-09: mechanical audit clean, fact-checked claim-by-claim against primary per-role logs (May 29-30) -- 1 real fix (a misquote, "your heads"->"our heads" in the PA writeup-reconstruction quote), 1 missing acronym gloss fixed (Comms self-reference), everything else confirmed solid including both directly-attributed PM quotes. Ready for PM voice-pass; frontmatter (image/alt/caption) still empty, needs filling before publish. PM voice-pass complete 2026-07-09 + frontmatter filled (ai-watchdog.png). Comms final editorial review same day: fixed 1 negation-reveal cliche (survived the voice-pass), 2 typos ("thier"->"their", "resistsance"->"resistance"), 1 missing word ("isn't unique [to] agentic development"), 2 trailing-whitespace lines. PA-writeup causal reframing (PM-initiated vs. self-discovered) checked against primary source and is a fair compression of PM's own account, not a factual error. Ready for Docs.

recentlyPublished.title: The Team Catches the Cycle
recentlyPublished.notes: Beat 11 (centerpiece). May 27 cohort rollout (9 of 11 roles) + May 28 worktree-default reversal / Model A / Mechanism-Beats-Vigilance; the architecture wrote its own case via 4 live clashes. Runs ~1990 words - PM voice-pass to tighten (Model A section most cuttable, marked inline). Footer teases Beat 12 (Jul 9).
recentlyPublished.altText: A warm editorial cartoon of several craftspeople crowding around one oversized workbench, accidentally interfering with one another despite working carefully, while a row of newly opened individual workbenches lets them spread out and continue building independently. A blueprint on the wall quietly guides newcomers setting up their own spaces.
recentlyPublished.caption: "I think we're getting the hang of this!"

imageMetadataGaps.title: Weekly Ship #049: The Team Builds Its Own Reliability
imageMetadataGaps.notes: Published 2026-07-01. hashId 406fba157dd4. Corrected: ADR-1312 removed; issue#s dropped from prose; frontmatter added.
imageMetadataGaps.altText: A child and a crew of robots checking each other's work on a boat.
